description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer who must scan existing Kubernetes manifests for container images that are still tagged “latest”.  
A colleague has placed two manifests in /home/user/manifests/.  Your job is to produce a compliance-scan report – nothing more – using awk and/or sed (no grep).  Keep the solution short (≈2-3 commands in total).

Detailed requirements the automated grader will verify:

1. Input files (already present):
   • /home/user/manifests/deployment.yaml  
   • /home/user/manifests/statefulset.yaml  

2. Report location you must create:
   • /home/user/policy_reports/latest_tag_report.log

3. Exact on-disk format of latest_tag_report.log  
   • One finding per line.  
   • Each line must have three colon-separated fields with NO extra whitespace:  
     <relative-filename>:<line-number>:<image-reference>  
     – relative-filename  → only the file name itself (no leading path)  
     – line-number        → the 1-based line number where the offending image appears  
     – image-reference    → the full image string that still contains “:latest”  
   • Lines must appear in lexicographic order by the relative-filename.  
   • A terminating newline is required at the end of the file.  

4. The final report must contain exactly the two lines below (the grader performs a diff):
   deployment.yaml:17:nginx:latest  
   statefulset.yaml:18:redis:latest  

5. Do NOT modify any other files or create extra files.  Only create the directory /home/user/policy_reports (if missing) and the file latest_tag_report.log inside it.

Example of correct file content (including final newline):
deployment.yaml:17:nginx:latest
statefulset.yaml:18:redis:latest⏎

Use only awk and/or sed in your pipeline (piping commands together is fine).  No grep, perl, python, etc.

Once the commands finish, the above report file must exist exactly as specified for the automated test to pass.
truth:
Initial file system state that exists when the task begins:

1. /home/user/manifests/deployment.yaml  
Contents (19 lines):
1  apiVersion: apps/v1
2  kind: Deployment
3  metadata:
4    name: web
5  spec:
6    replicas: 2
7    selector:
8      matchLabels:
9        app: web
10   template:
11     metadata:
12       labels:
13         app: web
14     spec:
15       containers:
16         - name: nginx
17           image: nginx:latest
18           ports:
19             - containerPort: 80

2. /home/user/manifests/statefulset.yaml  
Contents (20 lines):
1  apiVersion: apps/v1
2  kind: StatefulSet
3  metadata:
4    name: cache
5  spec:
6    serviceName: "redis"
7    replicas: 1
8    selector:
9      matchLabels:
10       app: cache
11   template:
12     metadata:
13       labels:
14         app: cache
15     spec:
16       containers:
17         - name: redis
18           image: redis:latest
19           ports:
20             - containerPort: 6379

No directory /home/user/policy_reports exists initially.

Expected artefact after the agent finishes:

File: /home/user/policy_reports/latest_tag_report.log  
Exact contents (including final newline character):
deployment.yaml:17:nginx:latest
statefulset.yaml:18:redis:latest

Permissions can remain default user umask values; no special ownership or modes required.
